species.notes: Dagang. Mixed dipterocarp forest, especially on ridges. 0-600m.

species.citation: Korth. , Verh. Nat. Gesch. Ned. Bezitt., Bot. (1841) 65 ; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 330. = Anisoptera mindanensis Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 181 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 92.
species.distribution: Borneo, Cambodia, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. MINDANAO: Zamboanga.
species.notes: Known from one record from Zamboanga. Primary lowland forest. 0-700m.

species.citation: (Blanco) Blume , Mus. Bot. 2 (1856) 42 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 92; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 333; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; Buhay et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 3085; ≡ Dipterocarpus thurifer (Blanco) Blanco , Fl. Filip., 2nd Ed. (1845) 310 ; Fl. Filip. ed. 3 2 (1878) 212; ≡ Mocanera thurifera Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 446. = Mocanera mayapis Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 449; ≡ Dipterocarpus mayapis (Blanco) Blanco , Fl. Filip., 2nd Ed. (1845) 212; ≡ Shorea mayapis (Blanco) Blume , Mus. Bot. 2 (1856) 33. = Anisoptera vidaliana Brandis , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 31 (1895) 44. = Anisoptera tomentosa Brandis , J. Linn. Philipp. (1904) 22. ssp. thurifera. = Anisoptera brunnea Foxw., PJS 6c (1911) 254; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 92; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 9; Type: FB 11292 Klemme (NY, US, iso), Luzon: Cagayan, Apr-1908. Notes: Palosapis. 0-750m. Native.
species.distribution: Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. BABUYAN ISLS, BOHOL, CALAYAN, CEBU, DINAGAT, HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Ecija (photos), Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Tarlac, Zambales, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Surigao, Zamboanga, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, TICAO.
species.notes: Dominant in evergreen and semi-evergreen primary forests; also occurring in some types of secondary forests. 0-750m.

species.citation: Korth. , Verh. Nat. Gesch. Ned. Bezitt., Bot. (1841) 66 ; Slik, Plants of Southeast Asia (2009 onwards).
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, ?Philippines, Sumatra. ?LUZON: ?Ilocos Norte.
species.notes: Possibly represented by Iwatsuki, Murata & Gutierrez 944 (L) from Ilocos Norte, Mt Sicapoa Range, Mt Bubungkayo, 6-Sep-1975. 0-1200m.

species.citation: Roxb. ex G.Don , Gen. Hist. 1 (1831) 813 ; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 11; Type: Roxburgh s.n. (BR), Myanmar. = Dipterocarpus philippinensis Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 179 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 90; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 325.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Cambodia, India, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Abra, Bataan, Isabela (photos), Nueva Vizcaya.
species.notes: Hairy-leaf apitong. Primary mixed dipterocarp forest in seasonal areas. Rare.

species.citation: Pierre , Fl. Forest. Cochinch. 14 (1889) t.214; Type: from Vietnam. ssp. condorensis; P.S.Ashton, Gardens' Bulletin Singapore 67 (2015) 357. = Dipterocarpus caudatus Foxw., PJS 13c (1918) 177; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 89; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 305; Type: Alvarez FB 21193 (K, lecto), Luzon: Camarines, Barrio Hibatac. Distribution: Philippines, Vietnam. LUZON: Albay, Camarines, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN. Notes: Primary lowland forests. Local in everwet zone. DAO category: Vulnerable. Native.
species.distribution: Philippines, Vietnam. LUZON: Albay, Camarines, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO, MINDORO, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind., Eerste Bijv. (1861) 485 ; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 324. = Dipterocarpus basilanicus Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 179 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 88.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. BASILAN.
species.notes: Local, on undulating land in lowland primary dipterocarp forest. Rare. 0-700m.

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 224 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 89; Ashton, Fl. (1845) 314 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 91; ≡ Mocanera verniciflua Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 451. = Dipterocarpus fulvus Blume , Mus. Bot. 2 (1856) 37. = Dipterocarpus velutinus S.Vidal , Revis. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 59; Type: Vidal 80 (MA, syn), Luzon: Bulacan, Angat; Vidal 81 (MA, syn), Luzon: Morong, Bosoboso.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, India, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. GUIMARAS, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MARINDUQUE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Cotabato, Davao, Surigao, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, POLILLO.
species.notes: Panau. Often gregarious in seasonal semi-evergreen dipterocarp forest on red soils; becoming scattered, rare, and confined to fertile red soils in everwet areas. 0-800m.

species.citation: (Blanco) Blanco , Fl. Filip., 2nd Ed. (1845) 314 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 89; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 317; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 15; ≡ Mocanera grandiflora Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 451.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Borneo, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BASILAN, BILIRAN, BOHOL, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Apayao, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Norte, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MANICANI, MARINDUQUE, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Sur, Davao, Misamis, Surigao, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAUI (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Apitong. Often common and dominant in semi-evergreen lowland and medium elevation dipterocarp forests, on small islands and on coastal hills in less seasonal part. 0-700m.

species.citation: Blume , Fl. Javae Dipter. (1829) 22 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 89; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 306, Fig. 23, 24; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 17; Type: from Java. = Dipterocarpus subalpinus Foxw. , LPB 6 (1913) 1950. ≠ Dipterocarpus trinervis auct. non Blume ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 91.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. BASILAN, BILIRAN, BOHOL, LEYTE, LUZON: Cagayan, Camarines, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Surigao, Zamboanga, NEGROS, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Hasselt's panau. Primary forests at low and medium elevation. 0-1000m.

species.citation: King , J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 62(2) (1893) 93 ; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 305; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 19; Type: from Peninsular Malaysia (Malacca). = Dipterocarpus cuneatus Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 178 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 89; Type: FB 21469. = Dipterocarpus perturbinatus Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 177. = Dipterocarpus obconicus Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 178 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 90; Type: Elmer 13498.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. LUZON: Albay, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Sur, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao del Norte, Surigao, Surigao del Sur, Zamboanga, MINDORO, SAMAR, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: Malapanau. On lateritic soils in seasonally dry primary dipterocarp forests. Frequently gregarious. 0-800m.

species.citation: King , J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 62(2) (1893) 96 ; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 309; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 21; Type: from Malaysia. = Dipterocarpus speciosus Brandis , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 31 (1895) 38 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 90; Type: Vidal 2160 (K), Basilan.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. BASILAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Cagayan, Camarines, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, MINDANAO: Agusan, Davao, Surigao, Surigao del Sur, NEGROS, POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: Broad-winged apitong. Primary lowland forests, on undulating or flat land, especially near streams.

species.citation: P.S.Ashton , Gard. Bull. Singapore 67 (2015) 359; Type: Gaerlan & Sagcal PPI 10209 (K, holo), Tawi-Tawi: Seratang Languyan.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. TAWI-TAWI.

species.citation: Blume , Mus. Bot. 2 (1856) 36 ; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 301; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 23. = Dipterocarpus affinis Brandis , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 31 (1895) 31 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 88. = Dipterocarpus lasiopodus Perkins , Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 22 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 90. = Dipterocarpus warburgii Brandis , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 31 (1895) 32 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 91. = Dipterocarpus caudiferus Merr. , PJS 29 (1926) 398. = Dipterocarpus macrrorhinus Slooten , Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenzorg, 3rd Sér. 8 (1927) 300. = Dipterocarpus woodii Merr. , PJS 29 (1926) 398.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. BASILAN, CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Aurora, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Sur, Isabela, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Quezon, Rizal, MARINDUQUE, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Davao, Misamis Occidental, Surigao, Zamboanga, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: Hagakhak. Common and frequently gregarious both in primary and secondary forests. There is some evidence of hybridization with Dipterocarpus kunstleri in the Philippines. 0-300m.

species.citation: Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 180 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 90; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 326.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. LUZON: Camarines Norte.
species.notes: Lowland mixed dipterocarp forest. Resembles Dipterocarpus grandiflorus Blanco in fruit and Dipterocarpus gracilis Blume in the indumentum.

species.notes: Manggachapui. Common in semi-evergreen and evergreen forests. In most provinces in Luzon. 100-800m.

species.citation: Foxw. , PJS 6c (1911) 260 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 93; Gutierrez, Act. Manil. 4A (1968) 53; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 425.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BASILAN, MINDANAO: Zamboanga, SIBUTU, SULU ARCHIPELAGO.
species.notes: Primary lowland forests on undulating hills. 0-70m.

species.citation: (Foxw.) Slooten , Reinwardtia 3 (1956) 317 ; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 414; ≡ Balanocarpus brachypterus Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 195, as 'brachyptera' ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 101; Type: FB 21895 Villamil, Mindanao: Zamboanga, 18-May-1914.

species.notes: Primary lowland forests. Rare.

species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao, Cagayan, Ilocos, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, SAMAR.
species.notes: Narek. Primary lowland semi-evergreen forests. 0-330(-?)m.

species.citation: Elmer , LPB 4 (1912) 1469 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 94; Gutierrez, Act. Manil. 4A (1968) 32; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 397. = Hopea glutinosa Elmer , LPB 4 (1912) 1470; Type: Elmer 12289 (MO, iso), Sibuyan. ≠ Hopea pierrei auct. non Hance ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 94.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Locally common on red sticky volcanic soil along ridges, in seasonal semi-evergreen forest. Collections cited by Foxworthy and Merrill from islands other than Sibuyan belong to Hopea malibato. 600-700m.

species.citation: H.G.Gut. & Foxw. , Asia Life Sci. 26 (2017) 2; Type: Loher 12914 (UC, holo), Luzon: Rizal, Oriud, Mar-1912.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Rizal.

species.notes: Yakal-kaliot. Primary evergreen dipterocarp forests in aseasonal areas. 0-700m.

species.citation: Foxw. , PJS 6c (1911) 261 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 93; Gutierrez, Act. Manil. 4A (1968) 63; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 435.

species.notes: Yakal-saplungan. Common in semi-evergreen seasonal forests at low and medium elevation, often dominant.

species.notes: Almon. Primary forest on undulating hills at low elevation, on clay soil. According to Ashton (1982), particularly variable in Mindanao where the leaves are much smaller and the tomentum more sparse and scabrid in some trees. 0-1060m to 1250m (photos).

BABUYAN ISLS, BASILAN, BOHOL, CALAYAN, CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Tarlac, MARINDUQUE, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Norte, Misamis Occidental, Zamboanga, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, OLUTANGA, PALAUI (photos), POLILLO, SAMAL, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, SULU ARCHIPELAGO.
species.notes: White lauan. Common in primary lowland forests from semi-seasonal to everwet areas, in the former often semi-gregarious. In most provinces in Luzon. 0-985m.

species.citation: Foxw. , PJS 13c (1918) 189 ; Foxworthy, PJS 67 (1938) 296; Ashton, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 458; Ashton & Heckenhauer, Kew Bull. 77 (2022) 888. = Shorea gisok Foxw. , PJS 67 (1938) 294. ≠ Shorea balangeran auct. non (Korth.) Burck ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 96, as 'Shorea balangeran (Korth.) Dyer ex S.Vidal'. ssp. falciferoides; Fernando, Dipterocarps of the Philippines (2009) 43; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 95. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BASILAN, CEBU, DINAGAT, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Bulacan, Camarines, Pangasinan, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan, Surigao, Zamboanga, SAMAR, SIBUYAN. Notes: Yakal-yamban. In moderately seasonal to everwet areas. According to Ashton (1982), specimens from the more seasonal NE Luzon (Shorea falciferoides) have more or less smaller leaves than other Philippine collections (formerly named Shorea gisok). The latter is more or less confined to ridgetops in the everwet areas. 0-1000m. Native.

App.) (1880) 21, nom. illeg., non Dyer. = Vatica bureavi F.Heim, Bull. Mens. Soc. Linn. Paris, 1st Ser. 2 (1891) 955; ≡ Sunaptea bureavi (F.Heim) F.Heim, Rech. Dipterocarp. (1892) 114. = Vatica whitfordii Foxw., PJS 67 (1938) 322. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Vietnam. BABUYAN ISLS, BASILAN, BOHOL, CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES, LEYTE, LUZON, MINDANAO: Agusan, Cotabato, Davao, Lanao, Surigao, Zamboanga, OLUTANGA, PANAY, SAMAR. Notes: Narig. Primary forests. In most provinces in Luzon. 0-800m. DAO category: Vulnerable. Native. ssp. obtusifolia (Elmer) P.S.Ashton, Gard. Bull. Singapore 31 (1978) 23; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1982) 365; ≡ Vatica obtusifolia Elmer, LPB 4 (1912) 1471; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 102; Type: Elmer 12963. Distribution: Borneo, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Zambales, PALAWAN. Notes: Primary lowland forests. According to Ashton, a species with much local variation especially in the Philippines. Forms approaching both Vatica odorata ssp. mindanensis and Vatica pachyphylla occur and suggest hybridization. DAO category: Endangered. Native.
